items.structure: with a difference of [X] percentage points
items.example: with a difference of 5.1 percentage points

items.structure: [X] surpassed [Y] in [sth]
items.example: Women surpassed men in several age groups

items.structure: [X] spend significantly more time than [Y] on [activity]
items.example: men spend significantly more time than women on TV, video, and radio

items.structure: [X] spend more time than [Y] on [sth]
items.example: Regarding leisure activities, men spend more time than women in all three categories.

items.structure: [X] spend slightly less time [activity] than [Y]
items.example: Men also spend slightly less time reading, with 18 minutes compared to 19 minutes for women

items.structure: [X] spend just [amount] minutes, while [Y] spend [amount] minutes
items.example: For sports activities, men spend 15 minutes daily, while women spend just 11 minutes.

items.structure: [X] spend substantially more time than [Y] in [sth]
items.example: When it comes to household activities, women spend substantially more time than men in all listed tasks.

items.structure: Regarding [category], [X]
items.example: Regarding leisure activities, men spend significantly more time than women.

items.structure: In [category], [X] allocate [amount] minutes, compared to [amount] for [Y]
items.example: In shopping, women allocate 35 minutes, compared to 25 minutes for men.

items.structure: The bar chart illustrates [sth]
items.example: The bar chart illustrates the proportion of Australian men and women across various age groups who participated in regular physical activity in 2010.

items.structure: approximately [X] minutes compared to [Y] minutes for [group]
items.example: approximately 35 minutes compared to 25 minutes for men

items.structure: [X] were more physically active ([percentage]%) than [Y] ([percentage]%)
items.example: men were more physically active (52.8%) than women (47.7%)

items.structure: [X] spend [amount] minutes per day on [sth]
items.example: Women spend 75 minutes per day on cooking and washing.

items.structure: Overall, the data reveals that [sth]
items.example: Overall, the data reveals that physical activity levels were highest among middle-aged Australians.

items.structure: The provided data compares [X] in [year], highlighting [Y]
items.example: The provided data compares the time spent by UK males and females on household and leisure activities in 2008, highlighting marked differences in their daily time allocation.

items.structure: Overall, the data presents a clear contrast in [sth]
items.example: Overall, the data presents a clear contrast in how UK males and females allocate their time between leisure and household activities.

items.structure: In conclusion, the data reveals that [X] showed distinct patterns in [Y]
items.example: In conclusion, the data reveals that UK men and women in 2008 showed distinct patterns in time spent on activities

items.structure: [X] generally spend more time on [sth], particularly [specific activities]
items.example: Women generally spend more time on household chores, particularly cooking and washing.

items.structure: [X] illustrates distinct patterns in [Y]
items.example: These trends illustrate distinct patterns in their daily routines.

items.structure: Participation rates experienced a decline for [X] after [Y]
items.example: Participation rates experienced a decline for both genders after the age of 54

items.structure: Physical activity rates increased for [X] in [Y]
items.example: Physical activity rates increased for both genders in the 45-54 age group
